Query: Coefficient of thermal expansion of which of following is most similar to that of tooth?

Options:
A: Gold inlay
B: Acrylic resin
C: Silicate cement
D: Gold foil
Retrieved Documents:
Document [0] (Title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Clinical Significance) Glass ionomer cement (GIC) is also known as man-made dentin because it simulates dentin in terms of modulus of elasticity, resilience, coefficient of thermal expansion, and thermal conductivity. GIC chemically adheres to the tooth structure, is biologically compatible, and has fluoride-releasing properties. [4] [5] These features proclaim GIC an excellent substitute for dentin. [7] However, reduced strength, abrasion resistance, and esthetics contribute to its decreased usage in various clinical scenarios. [4] [5] The material selection for a clinical condition should be made after weighing the merits and demerits of the available materials.
Document [1] (Title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern -- Physical Properties) The thermal expansion coefficient of GIC is similar to enamel and dentin. The thermal diffusivity of GIC is similar to dentin, providing thermal insulation to the pulp. The solubility of GIC in water is higher than zinc phosphate and zinc polycarboxylate. [14] GICs are tooth-colored restorative materials but are not recommended to restore anterior teeth because of their opacity and lack of translucency.
Document [2] (Title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Technique or Treatment -- Adjacent Stainless Steel Crown With Arch Length Loss/Space Loss) Reduction of the crown's mesiodistal dimension is frequently required, particularly when mesial drift (often caused by caries) has resulted in arch length loss. Flattening the crown's mesial and distal contact areas using Adam pliers results in a moderate reduction in the mesiodistal dimension. A contralateral upper tooth crown with a smaller size can be used if there is mesial drift in the lower arch. Another form of modification is vertical slicing and spot-welding, an additional segment of a stainless steel band. This is done to increase the perimeter or extend the length. However, the efficacy of the latter modifications remains largely untested. [8]
Document [3] (Title: Zirconia Biomaterials -- Issues of Concern) One of the technical complications reported in the YTZ-P reconstructions is chipping or delamination of the veneering porcelain. The core-veneer interface is a critical factor in the success of layered restorations. The contributing factors for the fracture may include weakness of the veneer material or the core/veneer bond, stresses and distortion due to the veneering process, unsupported veneering porcelain and configuration of the core and veneer, residual stresses arising from a coefficient of thermal expansion mismatch, and rapid cooling rates after heat treatment. The use of high-speed polishing rotary instruments is contraindicated for clinical use because of nickel contamination, phase changes in zirconia, and the resultant surface roughness.

Document [5] (Title: Hydroxyapatite Dental Material -- Issues of Concern) In contrast, the solid-state reaction yields a stoichiometric and well-defined crystalline structure of hydroxyapatite. However, this method requires high temperatures and extended heat treatment procedures. The raw materials used in the solid-state method should have a Ca/P ratio of 1.67 and must be ball-milled to ensure uniform particle size. [2] The solid-state reaction method solely depends on the solid diffusion of ions into the raw materials, thereby necessitating temperatures around 1250 ºC to initiate the reaction. [2] Moreover, prolonged heat treatment transforms singular crystalline particles into more blocky crystals, and the increase in crystalline size leads to a decrease in porosity, which is associated with the aging process. Document [6] (Title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Clinical Significance) Primary teeth are relatively at a larger risk of caries than permanent teeth. They also have larger pulp with prominent pulp horns, which places exact demands on cavity design. Hence, a full-coverage restoration is required. Although stainless steel crowns have an aesthetic disadvantage, they are still the treatment of choice for the following reasons: Durability Efficiency Longevity Cost-effectiveness Reliability Even though other tooth-colored restorations are available in the market, they are costly or require more tooth reduction, increasing the chances of pulp exposure. [15] [29]

Document [9] (Title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Issues of Concern) The longevity of glass ionomer cement restorations is affected by occlusal forces, porosities in set cement, desiccation or water absorption during initial setting, and use of the mixed cement after the loss of gloss. Furthermore, larger GIC particles decrease the wear rate; the cement's solubility depends on the amount of cement at the margins.
Document [10] (Title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Technique -- Core Obturation Materials) The warm vertical compaction technique uses a master gutta-percha cone, Schilder pluggers, and a heat source. [42] [40] [42] The master cone in this method should fit just short of the working length and have a slight tug back. After confirming with a radiograph, the canal is irrigated and dried, and sealer is applied to the walls. The master cone is reinserted, and the coronal portion is removed with a heated instrument. [42] Next, a heated plugger is used to gradually and sequentially remove portions of the coronal gutta-percha while softening the cone below. [42] The heated plugger is removed from the canal, a non-heated plugger is inserted, and the warm gutta-percha is compacted to force the now-plasticized material apically. [42] This process is repeated until 4 to 5 mm of adapted gutta-percha remains at the apex, forming an effective seal. [40] Small gutta-percha cones are inserted into the canal, heated with a heated plugger, and then compacted with a non-heated plugger. This is repeated until the canal is fully obturated with plasticized gutta-percha. [5] [42] [5] [39]
Document [11] (Title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Technique -- Core Obturation Materials) Since there are numerous types of obturation materials, there are several ways to insert them into the root canal, and as a result, this section focuses primarily on the insertion technique of gutta-percha. Gutta-percha has 2 main obturation techniques, cold lateral condensation and warm vertical compaction. [5] [39] The cold lateral condensation technique involves the selection of a master gutta-percha cone, the use of spreaders, and the insertion of secondary gutta-percha cones. [5] [40] The master cone is a gutta-percha cone with a taper and diameter that matches the prepared root canal at the working length. This master cone is measured to the working length and inserted into the canal, where it displays slight resistance to removal, aptly referred to as "tug back" if the correct size cone is selected. [5] After confirming with a radiograph, the canal is irrigated and dried, and sealer is applied to the walls. At this point, a finger spreader or a hand spreader is pre-fit in the canal to reach within 1 to 2 mm short of the working length, and appropriately sized secondary gutta-percha cones are selected that closely match the size of the spreader. [39]

Document [30] (Title: Glass Ionomer Cement -- Clinical Significance) GIC and its modifications have a wide array of uses. Owing to resiliency, very low shrinkage, and thermal compatibility with tooth structure, these materials have proven to be liners underneath restorative materials like dental composites (sandwich technique). [28] Attributing to the property of bonding to dentin surfaces without removing the smear layer, their biological compatibility, and fluoride release, GICs and modified GICs are the materials of choice in restoring carious teeth in high-risk patients. [29] Because of the ease of placement and snap set property, light-cured GICs are the material of choice for restoration in children. [7]
Document [31] (Title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Technique -- Core Obturation Materials) The master cone is inserted into the canal to the working length, followed by the spreader, which, if properly selected, should reach 2 mm shorter. The spreader is then rotated and withdrawn from the canal, and the matching gutta-percha cone is inserted into the vacant space. [39] The process is repeated until the operator cannot insert the spreader deeper than the coronal third of the prepared canal. The excess gutta-percha is seared off with a heated instrument and compacted using a root canal plugger. [5] [41] [39]

Document [13] (Title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 4 -- Community 2902) The concept “Without” is associated with قيلة مستقيمية.
Document [14] (Title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 3 -- Community 2089) Burns are medical injuries that have a direct relationship with حرق حراري, indicating thermal burns, and are commonly associated with جرح حروق, which refers to burn wounds; recognizing these connections is essential for accurate clinical assessment and effective management of burn-related injuries.
Document [15] (Title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2082) Crushing injuries or internal injuries are associated with open wounds (جرح مفتوح), indicating that such traumatic forces often result in visible tissue disruption.
Document [16] (Title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1804) خديج يحدث نتيجة متلازمة خلل نضج الرئة، حيث يُعَدّ اضطراب نضج الرئة الأساسي المسبب لتطور خديج.
Document [17] (Title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2329) Infancy is identified as a specific developmental stage that belongs to the broader category of the period of life between birth and death, situating this early phase within the overall lifespan continuum.
Document [18] (Title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1494) A gestational age of 29‑30 weeks completed is associated with a liveborn infant, indicating that pregnancies reaching this stage can result in a live birth.
Document [19] (Title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2430) Other miscellaneous procedures are classified as a subset of other nonoperative procedures, indicating that they fall under the broader category of nonoperative interventions.
Document [20] (Title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 2678) الإجراءات العلاجية الخاصة بالمريء تشمل ربط دوالي المريء كإجراء فرعي ضمن مجموعة العلاجات الموجهة للمريء.
Document [21] (Title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 1838) Charcoal preparations that are used as intestinal absorbents are classified as members of the activated charcoal group.
Document [22] (Title: UMLS -- Run 27 -- Level 3 -- Community 2407) Dilation of genitourinary system is classified as a dilation procedure.
Document [23] (Title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 3 -- Community 1701) Respiratory failure; insufficiency; arrest (adult) is a specific adult respiratory condition that is classified under the broader category of اضطراب تَنَفُّسِيّ (respiratory disorder).
Document [24] (Title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 4 -- Community 2849) Fat emulsion is a type of dietary supplement.
Document [25] (Title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 1440) إدمان الهيروين يُصنَّف كنوع من الاعتماد على الأدوية، أي أن تعاطي الهيروين يُعد حالة إدمان تتضمن الاعتماد الجسدي والنفسي على المخدر.
Document [26] (Title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 3 -- Community 2453) The areola is identified as a breast part, representing a specific anatomical component of the breast.
Document [27] (Title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2591) ربط دوالي المريء (الربط الجراحي لدوالي المريء) يرتبط مباشرةً بخصائص مورفولوجية وريد متدلي، مما يدل على أن هذا الإجراء يهدف إلى معالجة الوريد المتدلي أو المتوسع في جدار المريء.
Document [28] (Title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2431) Cervical shortening in pregnancy is categorized as a component of other complications of birth and puerperium that influence the management of the mother, and it specifically encompasses the sub‑condition of cervical shortening occurring in the second trimester.
Document [29] (Title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 3 -- Community 1409) Calcium citrate is a chemical compound that is derived from citric acid, with citric acid serving as its parent molecule.
Document [30] (Title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 1510) Corticosteroid ophthalmologic and otologic preparations are a specific type of ophthalmological and otological preparations.
Document [31] (Title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 4 -- Community 2885) COMPLICATIONS MAINLY RELATED TO PREGNANCY is classified as a subcategory within the broader group of Complications of pregnancy, childbirth and the puerperium, indicating that these health issues are primarily associated with the gestational period and its immediate aftermath.
